primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, en comptes consolidés, du modèle de comptabilité de couverture IFRS potentiellement applicable à la composante change de dividendes intragroupe, selon que l’élément couvert est une transaction intragroupe prévue ou une créance intragroupe reconnue.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the required strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge-accounting eligibility and model questions, so IFRS 9 governs and IAS 39 is discarded from the candidate set.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IFRS 9 directly addresses hedge-accounting models and the consolidated intragroup hedged-item exceptions relevant here, while IAS 39 is legacy overlapping guidance.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern which intragroup items may be hedged in consolidated financial statements and identify the named hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify when foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items survive consolidation and when such items are treated as part of a net investment.
This Interpretation constrains and clarifies the net investment hedge model, including eligible hedged risk and where the hedging instrument may be held within the group.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting models for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements
Eligible intragroup hedged items in consolidation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows, including highly probable forecast transactions and qualifying intragroup foreign currency exposures.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 21 together establish hedge of a net investment as a separate named hedge accounting model for qualifying foreign operation exposures within a group.
